key_insight: The paper proves that gradient descent can converge near stationary points for broad neural network architectures beyond the NTK regime by combining analyticity-based PL inequalities with trajectory-dependent smoothness.
engineering_relevance: It gives practical learning-rate scaling guidance for modern architectures like pre-normalized transformers, emphasizing depth and bottleneck dimensions over the largest width.
method: Establish an iterate-dependent PL-type inequality using analyticity and measure-zero arguments.
Prove Lipschitz smoothness along the GD path via polynomial generalized smoothness and a local relaxed dissipative condition.
Interpret the theory under Xavier initialization and architectural scaling to derive learning-rate and nondegeneracy implications.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical convergence framework and proofs under mild assumptions, but no empirical dataset benchmark or quantitative experiment results.
watch_item: The guarantees are asymptotic and assumption-driven, so applicability depends on whether the architecture and initialization satisfy the stated analyticity, smoothness, and genericity conditions.
paper_title: Convergence of Gradient Descent for General Neural Network Architectures Beyond the NTK Regime
